title: West Nile Virus found in mosquito test sample in Jamaica Plain
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A mosquito test sample in Jamaica Plain tested positive for West Nile Virus, the first such case in Boston this summer.
Boston Public Health Commission advises precautions despite low transmission risk; no human cases confirmed in the city.
Regular mosquito sampling will continue through summer and early fall to monitor potential West Nile Virus spread.

title: Boston man accused of making homemade explosives, threats on MBTA Commuter Rail
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Shane Cautillo, 29, was charged with possession of hoax devices and unlawful possession of fireworks on June 29.
Cautillo allegedly made bomb threats on an MBTA Commuter Rail, referencing U.S. Capitol attacks, according to a witness.
Explosive components and fireworks were found with Cautillo's belongings; no injuries occurred from a resultant explosion.

title: Lawsuit: ICE detained East Boston father despite legal status
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jose Pineda, 62, an East Boston resident with legal status, is suing ICE over alleged unlawful detention in 2025.
The lawsuit claims racial profiling and cruel detention conditions, including overcrowding, and lack of basic hygiene supplies.
Despite prior humanitarian protections, ICE initiated and later dismissed removal proceedings against Pineda after his release.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Running backs edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson lead a Patriots rushing attack that ranked sixth in the NFL last season.
TreVeyon Henderson set a franchise rookie record with 5.1 yards per carry; Stevenson contributed 12 plays of 20 yards or more.
Patriots have depth concerns at running back, drafting Jam Miller in the seventh round to add potential support.

title: Boston cyclist, a city employee, hit and killed by vehicle on Tremont Street
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A cyclist, identified as Louisa Gag, was fatally struck by a vehicle on Tremont Street near Roxbury Crossing Thursday morning.
The incident occurred around 8:20 a.m. at 1457 Tremont St.; the driver stopped and police are investigating.
Tremont Street was closed from Brigham Circle to Roxbury Crossing until noon following the crash, Boston police reported.

title: Massachusetts Senate passes bill targeting addictive social media features for kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Senate passed a bill requiring social media firms to disable addictive features for minors by default.
The bill mandates disabling autoplay, algorithmic feeds, and push notifications for children from 10 p.m. to 6 a.m.
Legislation includes privacy protections for minors and requires annual reports on how platforms target them.

title: Police seize dog who severely injured smaller pup in Chelsea attack caught on video
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Chelsea police seized an unlicensed dog that attacked and injured a smaller service dog on Chestnut Street.
The injured dog, a service animal named Snoopy, requires leg amputation due to the attack.
A pet owner may face local ordinance and state law violations; a dangerous dog hearing is possible.

title: Mac Jones opens up about his tumultuous time with the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mac Jones was traded from the New England Patriots to Jacksonville for a sixth-round pick.
Jones described confusion and issues with offensive play-calling following Josh McDaniels's departure.
He felt relieved after the trade to Jacksonville and views it as a fresh start.

title: 5 takeaways as France defeat Morocco in the last ‘Boston Stadium’ World Cup game, advance to semifinals
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 at Gillette Stadium in the World Cup quarterfinals to advance to the semifinals.
Goals from Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembélé secured the victory for France against a strong Moroccan defense.
The match was the last at 'Boston Stadium' during the tournament, marking the end of its World Cup hosting duties.

title: ‘He hit me’: Pregame France vs. Morocco press conference briefly halted after journalist altercation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: A pregame press conference for the France vs. Morocco World Cup quarterfinal was interrupted by a journalist altercation.
FIFA officials intervened as two journalists argued during Moroccan player Brahim Díaz's media session at Boston Stadium.
The press conference incident involved no apparent connection to the teams; French coach Didier Deschamps faced separate media tensions.
